name: Ducklings Swimming in a Pond
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the mushroom mixture: Wash and soak the mushrooms in warm water for 1 hour; slice into thin strips and reserve.
2. Blanch the shrimp in salted boiling water (omit if shrimp are precooked).
3. Separate egg yolks and discard for another recipe.
4. Beat the egg whites until stiff but not runny, starting slowly and gradually increasing speed once the eggs begin to foam.
5. Form two ducklings per serving by flattening an egg in a spoon and turning it over onto a steaming plate.
6. Add a blob of egg for the neck and head, another piece for the tail using a toothpick to carefully shape the head and tail.
7. Steam one duckling as a test; steaming should take 30-60 seconds to set the surface of the egg.
8. After steaming, use a toothpick to dab yellow food coloring on the beak and tail.
9. Just before serving, heat the broth just under boiling point and add the reserved mushrooms and peas.
10. Add the shrimp and desired seasonings 5 minutes before serving.
11. Pour the hot soup into a warm serving bowl and float the prepared ducklings in the soup.
Serves: 4
